name: Raising First In
description: This is when no one has entered the pot before you and you decide to raise. You are the first player to put in more than the big blind.

name: 3-Bet
description: This is when a player raises and you raise again after them. It shows strength or can be used to push them out of the pot.

name: 4-Bet
description: This happens when there is a raise, a 3-bet, and then you raise again. It usually means very strong hands or a big bluff.

name: Cold Call
description: This is when a player has already raised and you just call without raising. You enter the pot without increasing the bet.

name: Set Mining
description: This is when you call with a small pocket pair hoping to hit three of a kind on the flop. You usually do this when stacks are deep enough to win a big pot.

name: Isolation Raise
description: This is when someone limps and you raise to play against them alone. The goal is to get heads-up with a weaker player.

name: Limp
description: This is when you just call the big blind instead of raising. You enter the pot cheaply without showing strength.

name: Overcall
description: This is when one player raises, another calls, and you also call. You join the pot without raising after multiple players are already in.

name: Squeeze
description: This is when there is a raise and one or more callers, and you make a big re-raise. The goal is to force everyone to fold and win the pot immediately.

name: Steal
description: This is when you raise from a late position to try to win the blinds. You are taking advantage of players who have not entered the pot.

name: Defending the Blinds
description: This is when you are in the big blind or small blind and decide to call or raise against a steal. You protect your blind from being taken too easily.
